title: The RLHF Pipeline
concept: RLHF uses the reward model's feedback to tune the LLM's weights using reinforcement learning. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Reward Modeling.'

exercises.prompt: What does the acronym RLHF stand for?
exercises.explain: RLHF stands for Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback. It uses human preferences to guide AI behavior.

cards.title: Bridging the Gap
cards.body: We have an LLM, and we have a Reward Model that rates how good its answers are. But how do we actually update the LLM so it gets better? That is where the RLHF pipeline connects the dots!

cards.title: The RLHF Pipeline
cards.body: The RLHF Pipeline is the process of taking a base AI, scoring its responses using a simulator of human preferences (the Reward Model), and updating the AI's internal settings using Reinforcement Learning so it chooses better words in the future.

cards.title: The Puppy Trainer
cards.body: Imagine training a puppy! You can't just tell it what to do in human language. Instead, you wait for it to do something good, give it a treat (the reward), and over time the puppy changes its behavior to get more treats. The LLM changes its 'brain' (weights) based on the reward model's digital treats.

cards.title: Adjusting on the Fly
cards.body: If an LLM is asked to write a polite email, it might draft two versions. The reward model scores the polite version high (+2.0) and the rude version low (-1.5). The RL pipeline then adjusts the LLM's internal weights to make it more likely to choose the polite phrasing next time.

cards.title: Myth: Humans Watch Every Step
cards.body: You might think human judges have to sit and score every single output during the final training stage. Actually, humans only train the Reward Model once. The Reward Model then automatically scores millions of practice runs without humans needing to watch!

cards.title: The Pipeline Shift
cards.body: Compare how a model changes before and after the RL update step in the pipeline.

right.text: Adjusts its vocabulary weights to consistently output high-reward, helpful answers.

left.text: Outputs answers randomly, sometimes polite, sometimes rude or useless.

exercises.prompt: Which training method uses the reward model's feedback to update the LLM's settings?
exercises.explain: Reinforcement Learning (RL) acts as the engine that updates the model's internal settings based on the reward scores.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The RLHF pipeline allows the AI to train automatically using the Reward Model instead of needing live human feedback for every single update.
exercises.explain: The Reward Model acts as a proxy for human feedback, automatic scoring outputs so we don't need real humans grading millions of trials in real time.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how RLHF updates the model.
exercises.sentence: The RL algorithm updates the LLM's internal weights to maximize the score from the reward model.
exercises.explain: Reinforcement learning algorithms use the reward scores to nudge the AI's internal weights so it outputs better answers.

exercises.prompt:
Which response is more likely from an LLM successfully updated by an RLHF pipeline?

Response A: "Figure it out yourself, I don't care."
Response B: "I can certainly help you with that! Let's start with step one."
exercises.explain: Response B is generated by an AI using RLHF because it actively tries to be helpful and safe, whereas Response A shows toxic behavior typical of untuned models.

exercises.prompt: How does the RLHF pipeline actually use the reward model to improve the LLM?

options.text: It generates new training data and restarts training from scratch
It uses reinforcement learning algorithms to adjust the LLM's weights based on the reward score
It asks humans to manually change the code of the LLM every second
It forces the LLM to only copy and paste pre-written human answers

exercises.explain: By using the reward model as a guide, RL algorithms change the model's internal connections so it speaks more like a helpful human and less like a chaotic text generator. Next up: Reward Modeling.
